creator: sysseh@devetir.qld.gov.au
text:
When using scanf in a program that fiddles with its stack using alloca, and
invalid data is entered for an integer field, the system will hang. It
responds to pings, and keystrokes at the console, but telnet/rlogin sessions
do not echo key strokes, nor does the X server continue to operate.

Fix: 

Unknown at this stage.
How-To-Repeat: 
Unpack the following tar file into a directory, cd into the cs350 directory,
and type "gmake simeg". Run the simeg program, enter 2 for the number of
servers, "N" for debugging, "0" for the random number seed and "]" for the
"Samples Needed". The machine should immediately hang.

(For those that are curious, this is part of a Simulation subject I did a
few years ago. I pulled it out to see if the disgusting things I did to
simulate coroutines on a set of System 5 machines would work under FreeBSD,
as the pthreads library does not seem to be about anymore.)
